reviews.review_description:
Great app. Great documentary. It was a real experience. Unlike the free Notre Dame app from Ubisoft, this app gets the light streaming in from the stained glass windows hitting the wall just right. The other app had just plain yellow squares of sunlight on the wall. Imagine that.
Looking at even pictures of light from the stained glass windows hitting the wall on Sunday afternoons and listening to soft gentle 70s rock music is magical and brings intense Sunday vibes.
Thank you for this app which deserves at least 15 stars out of 10.

Deuteronomy 31:8
The Lord is the One who goes before you. He will never leave you nor forsake you. Do not worry nor be afraid.

reviews.review_title: A great tour through space and time
reviews.review_description:
Seeing what the cathedral might have looked like throughout it's history gives you greater appreciation for what a marvel it is, both artistically, and scientifically. This experience is superb. I'd love it if subtitles were added with more localizations, including English so I could enjoy the experience in French.
The app should by rights be on the main store, and I hope it will be soon. More people need to see this. Maybe Meta could make a big deal of it and throw in a Notre Dame home environment?

This is a special experience, really very well done. It makes me very excited about what VR can be. I already owned this on the other device it's available on, but I wanted to see the Quest version.

It's great to learn about the value and history of Notre Dame. I knew it's a very old building, but this really brought the history alive.

As far as the comparison goes: I believe this Quest version is the better experience. It's more stable and slightly better looking. So if you own both devices, get it on Quest.

There's a bug with there not being sound in chapter 11 (Foundations), because there is sound in the French translation. However this bug exists on the other version I experienced as well 

Regardless of that, I think this is a pretty unmissable experience for history buffs, Catholics, fans of classic architecture, France lovers and many more people besides. Very well done!
Amazing and educational. Worth every penny.

Very impressive! It shows what vr can deliver besides all we know so far. I enjoyed it and can only recommend it - it is more than worth the price!
Victor Hugo would have loved it.
